strategies.hypothesis: A single independent answer is the minimum-cost baseline.
strategies.name: Direct One

strategies.hypothesis: A small independent plurality is a stable low-cost baseline.
strategies.name: Five-Vote Plurality

strategies.hypothesis: Because the five-bank winner matched its candidate oracle without creating any new correct repairs, three falsifying reviewers that must choose from the same shuffled bank may preserve its corrections while reducing unsupported answer generation.
strategies.name: Five-Bank Falsifying Selection

strategies.hypothesis: The full nine-answer bank should improve generic candidate coverage while retaining the successful falsifying-selection organization; this tests whether the large oracle gap can be converted without answer-generating repair.
strategies.name: Full-Nine Falsifying Selection

strategies.hypothesis: One falsifying selector may retain a useful fraction of the three-reviewer panel's oracle recovery at six calls per activated case, directly measuring the marginal value of reviewer replication.
strategies.name: One-Review Five-Bank Selection

strategies.hypothesis: Restoring review-plurality fallback without a full-bank delegate vote will test whether the harmful reversal observed in Iteration 7 came from delegate fusion while preserving the orthogonal committee architecture and its positive correction balance.
strategies.name: Committee Comparison Fallback Replication
